items.title: US, Iran discuss 45-day ceasefire as mediators push for deal ahead of deadline
items.summary: The United States (US), Iran and regional mediators are discussing terms for a possible 45-day ceasefire that could lead to an end of fighting, Bloomberg reported on Monday, citing Axios. The people, who were not named, said chances of reaching a deal over the next 48 hours are low.

items.title: Asian stocks trade mixed amid Trump's fresh deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Asian equity markets opened mixed at the start of a new week as traders remain on edge amid the risk of a further escalation of tensions in the Middle East.

items.title: Australian Dollar softens on strong US jobs data, Middle East tensions
items.summary: The AUD/USD pair extends the decline to near 0.6885 during the early Asian session on Monday. The US Dollar (USD) strengthens against the Australian Dollar (AUD) amid stronger-than-expected US employment data and ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

items.title: Japanese Yen holds steady amid US-Iran tensions
items.summary: The USD/JPY pair trades on a flat note near 159.65 during the early Asian session on Monday. The pair steadies as traders continue to assess the developments surrounding the US-Iran conflict.

items.title: OPEC+ agrees to boost oil output in May amid global energy crisis
items.summary: The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and its allies (OPEC+) agreed on Sunday to raise their oil output quotas by 206,000 barrels per day (bps) for May, Reuters reported.

items.title: WTI rises to near $105.00 as Trump threatens to hit Iran infrastructure
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), the US crude oil benchmark, is trading around $105.00 during the Asian trading hours on Monday. The WTI price climbs to its highest levels in nearly four years amid escalating conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: US President Donald Trump: Open the Strait of you’ll be living in hell
items.summary: United States President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iran if they do not reopen the Strait of Hormuz before the deadline, in a post shared on Truth Social.

items.title: US S&P Global Services PMI posts first contraction since 2023
items.summary: The US S&P Global Services PMI in March reported that business activity in the sector is slowing sharply, falling to contractionary territory for the first time since January 2023, amid higher inflation and the war in the Middle East.

items.title: US President Trump: We can easily reopen the Hormuz Strait
items.summary: United States President Donald Trump is on the wires on Good Friday, claiming on Truth Social that the US can easily reopen the Strait of Hormuz, take the oil, and make a fortune.

items.title: Euro is looking for direction with all eyes on US Nonfarm Payrolls data
items.summary: The Euro (EUR) is trading sideways between 1.1530 and 1.1550 against the US Dollar (USD) in a holiday-thinned session, with most markets closed on Good Friday. The pair is on track for a 0.3% weekly appreciation, yet with price action trapped halfway through March’s trading range.

items.title: NZD/USD hovers above mid-term lows at 0.5700 with US NFP on tap
items.summary: The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) is showing the weakest performance among major currencies in an otherwise calm Good Friday session, with most markets closed on bank holidays.

items.title: WTI remains stronger near $104 as Trump threats fuel supply fears
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) oil price steadies around $103.80 per barrel during the early European hours on Friday. WTI price gained over 10% as supply concerns intensified following renewed Iran threats from US President Donald Trump.
